channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: The impact of floods on public health and governance in Kenya
item.description: Last week’s floods in Kenya reminded us once again that vulnerability to disasters isn’t equal. Poor households, people living in unstable housing, and areas with weak infrastructure are hardest hit. ...

item.title: Evidence Suggests Carrim and Maumela May Have Funded Matlala’s R360m Tender
item.description: At the Madlanga Commission, evidence leader Matthew Chaskalson presented findings suggesting that North West businessman Suliman Carrim and Hangwani Morgan Maumela may have funded Vusi Cat Matlala’s c...

item.title: Ex-Judge Mutava and Associates Granted Sh200,000 Bail in Alleged Sh10.4 Million Bribery Case
item.description: In Nairobi, former High Court judge Joseph Mutava together with three other suspects have been released on police cash bail of Sh200,000 each after their arrest by the Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commi...

item.title: Governor Oborevwori opens new 4.8km road to boost farming and local trade in Oko
item.description: On Wednesday, March 11, 2026, residents of Oko and nearby communities for Oshimili South Local Government Area, Delta State, celebrate as Governor Sheriff Oborevwori officially opens the 4.8-kilometre...

item.title: Rukwa Region to Receive Electricity from National Grid in May 2026
item.description: People in Rukwa Region are soon going to get electricity straight from the national grid, expected by May 2026 once the 400kV high-voltage line from Iringa is completed. Deputy Minister for Energy, Ms...

item.title: Health and Safety Inspectors Give Evidence in CFMEU Construction Union Inquiry
item.description: The CFMEU inquiry continues in Queensland, examining allegations of regulatory capture and misconduct within the construction union. Workplace Health and Safety Queensland inspectors have returned to ...

item.title: Tanzania Strengthens Efforts to Tackle Rare Diseases Through Research and Healthcare Improvements
item.description: Tanzania government is pushing stronger measures to handle the challenge of rare diseases, urging more collaboration between universities, hospitals and research institutions. Health officials warn th...

item.title: UK Government to Release Initial Documents on Lord Mandelson’s Ambassadorial Appointment
item.description: The UK government is set to release the first batch of internal documents concerning Lord Mandelson’s appointment as British Ambassador to the United States on Wednesday at lunchtime. The release will...

item.title: Jets struggle in 4-1 defeat against Anaheim Ducks
item.description: The Winnipeg Jets experienced a disappointing night against the Anaheim Ducks, falling 4-1 in a game that exposed struggles on both ends of the ice. Despite early optimism after earning points in thei...

item.title: Depu scores hat-trick as Yanga thrash Polisi; three top-flight clubs exit CRDB Cup
item.description: In the latest round of the CRDB Federation Cup, Young Africans S.C. striker Laurindo Dilson Aurélio, commonly known as Depu, scored a sensational hat-trick, helping his team defeat Polisi Tanzania 7–1...

item.title: Inquest opens into deaths of two teenagers from suspected carbon monoxide at holiday park
item.description: An inquest has been opened into the tragic deaths of Cherish Bean, 15, and Ethan Slater, 17, who were found dead in a cabin at Little Eden Holiday Park in Bridlington, Yorkshire. According to the inqu...

item.title: US military operations in Iran cost billions in first days
item.description: Washington reports that the US military kharcha on operations against Iran has already hit billions of dollars. During the opening days of the conflict, munition and missile interceptor costs alone ra...

item.title: Gauteng MEC Maile Tables R179.2 Billion Budget Focusing on Services, Infrastructure and Fiscal Discipline
item.description: Gauteng's MEC for Finance and Economic Development, Lebogang Maile, has presented a R179.2 billion budget for the 2026/27 financial year, aimed at enhancing frontline services, accelerating infrastruc...

item.title: CDC Introduces Shared Clinical Decisionmaking Approach for Certain Vaccines
item.description: Since Robert F. Kennedy Jr. took office as US Health and Human Services Secretary, his administration has made significant changes to the childhood vaccination schedule, shifting several vaccines from...

item.title: ICE Confirms El Paso Detention Facility to Remain Open with Upgraded Operations Under New Contractor
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) has confirmed that Camp East Montana, a detention facility located in El Paso, Texas, will remain operational despite previous reports suggesting it could fac...

item.title: Study Reveals How Neutron Star Mergers Spread Precious Metals Across the Universe
item.description: Astrophysicists have studied a cosmic event that took place 8.5 billion light-years away, where two neutron stars collided, producing a short gamma-ray burst known as GRB 230906A. These binary neutron...

item.title: Crikey seeks multimedia editor to expand video and audio journalism
item.description: Crikey, Australia’s leading independent news outlet, is on the hunt for a skilled multimedia editor to join its dynamic team. Following a successful 2025, which saw the launch of an election podcast a...

item.title: Tel Aviv residents hold dance parties in bomb shelters amid ongoing missile threats from Iran
item.description: In Tel Aviv, Israel's vibrant coastal city known for its lively nightlife, young people have adapted to the realities of the war with Iran by transforming designated bomb shelters into impromptu dance...

item.title: Drone attack targets US diplomatic support centre in Baghdad amid escalating US-Israel-Iran conflict
item.description: A drone struck the Baghdad Diplomatic Support Center, a key US logistical hub near Baghdad International Airport, as part of retaliatory actions by Iranian-backed Iraqi militias under the Islamic Resi...

item.title: Kerala Government Recognises Pentecostal Churches as Official Christian Denomination
item.description: The Kerala cabinet has approved in principle the recognition of Pentecostal churches as an official Christian denomination. This decision follows a meeting where a delegation of Pentecostal church lea...
